name: Jay Inslee
subject: Courage means doing what's hard
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
After the horrific violence in Las Vegas, we must act -- together -- to renew the Assault Weapons Ban.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTIwNzYyMTYS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDM0NTU2MgS2&mt=1&rt=0) Dear Friend, It's difficult for me conceive of the horrific violence suffered in Las Vegas Sunday night. Trudi and I, and all Washingtonians, pray for the hundreds wounded and those fighting now for their lives, and we grieve for the 59 individuals killed. While we pray, we're also in awe of the actions of the first responders who showed incredible courage. And we are inspired by the heroic stories of strangers protecting those near them during the shooting. We draw hope from these stories. One thing I know during these dark chapters in our American story is that our actions define us. We saw that from the first responders and everyday citizens Sunday night. Next, we must see that same courage from the rest of us -- even when it's hard. One critical place to start: Congress should reinstate the Assault Weapons Ban. I voted for that ban as a member of Congress. And when politicians in Washington D.C. allowed the Assault Weapons Ban to expire, it was an egregious abdication of leadership. Now is the time to fix that mistake. Three nights ago in Las Vegas, we saw what American courage and bravery looks like in the face of incredible danger. Now, our representatives in D.C. must show a sliver of that courage and act. [**Today, I 'm joining with millions of Americans to call on Congress to renew the Assault Weapons Ban -- add your name and join the call for action.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIwNzYyMTYS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDM0NTU2MgS2&mt=1&rt=0) The vote I took back in 1994 for gun safety reform cost me my seat in Congress, yet I have never once regretted it. I'd take that vote again and again if I could. Yesterday, a few hundred Republican lawmakers tweeted their "thoughts and prayers" for the victims in Las Vegas while many of them took thousands of dollars from the gun lobby for their campaigns. The same gun lobby that fights against all common sense gun safety measures. So let me be clear: In this dark time, we will be judged by our actions. Courage means standing up for what's right, even when it's hard. Even when it means going against a big donor. Last year, after the horrific attack in Orlando, Americans in states including Washington and California voted at the ballot overwhelmingly for common sense gun safety measures. Nevada also passed gun safety legislation by popular vote. But gun violence is a national issue -- it touches Americans in every corner of the country -- and it demands a national solution. Renewing the Assault Weapons Ban is just one step towards common sense gun safety in America. But it's an important step. Actions matter. We saw that in Las Vegas and now we must see if from Congress. We pray. We hope. And now we must act. Please join me here: [**jayinslee.com/gunsafety**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIwNzYyMTYS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDM0NTU2MgS2&mt=1&rt=0) Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTIwNzYyMTYS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDM0NTU2MgS2&mt=1&rt=0)
---

name: The White House
subject: President Trump meets with the brave heroes of Las Vegas
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=54c7cfe28911dcda0b708f7b44e18ba1c0466165dc9985611bbeddea76f0ed8abcf8d87aa243071d75689976588ed4b1ce26a35bea3701f9

The President and First Lady visit with the brave heroes of Las Vegas
On Wednesday morning, President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump traveled to Las Vegas, Nevada, to visit with survivors of the Sunday night shooting and local medical professionals. The President and First Lady then met with civilian heroes and first responders. President Trump gave remarks at the Las Vegas Metropolitan Police Department, commending city officials and first responders for their diligent work and offering words of solidarity to the bereaved. “When the worst of humanity strikes, and strike it did, the best of humanity responds,” the President said.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=54c7cfe28911dcdadd266962aa3ce7855d6df7d5cd9a3c0323bca3a260947882ecd6d7320aa06294d4148bfb13cb3d01ca82bd6502d11461
See More

May God Bless the People of Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands
On Tuesday, First Lady Melania Trump accompanied the President to Puerto Rico to visit with individuals impacted by Hurricane Maria. The First Lady, along with President Trump, surveyed the damage and relief efforts and met personally with hurricane victims. “To the first responders and local officials who have worked tirelessly to provide aide, on behalf of a grateful Nation I say thank you. To our fellow citizens in Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands, I continue to offer you daily thoughts and prayers. May God bless the people of Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands and offer comfort in this time of loss,” said the First Lady.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=54c7cfe28911dcda7083b913ca87b37747e5d5273fea670084ef3e36f76c2d511e1a7307040f8de649bf058ae80fb259533d231b7af9160a
Watch the video here

Update on FEMA’s Hurricane Recovery Response
Our goal is to help Puerto Ricans return to their routines, which will progress at a faster rate as more businesses open and communities are able to support their residents. We continue to make progress through the unified efforts of the federal government, working hand-in-hand with Puerto Rico, its municipalities, businesses and voluntary agencies on the island.

FEMA and its federal partners will be here for years to come, helping Puerto Rico not only in its response, but also its recovery. For more information, and to apply for assistance if you have been impacted by the storm, check out the resources available to you
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=54c7cfe28911dcda1f83bc85cf10b4f3db8c0ec907c13647c3bce8576dfce63dd441197277ba1a9c11373659cc87d38b89b3c2b73bd41e77
here.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump meet with Las Vegas Metropolitan Police Officer Brady Cook | October 4, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS AND VP TODAY

Today, Vice President Pence hosted the first meeting of the National Space Council at the Steven F. Udvar-Hazy Center in Chantilly, Virginia. In the afternoon, Vice President Pence will travel to Florida to receive a briefing on hurricane recovery efforts and to tour a Disaster Relief Center. This evening, President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump will meet with senior military leaders and their spouses at the White House.

COMING UP

Tomorrow, the President and First Lady will host an event in honor of Hispanic Heritage Month at the White House. The Vice President will depart Florida en route to Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands to meet with FEMA and survey affected sites of Hurricane Maria.

For more information, and to apply for assistance if you have been impacted by the storm, check out the resources available to you here. Photo of the Day President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump meet with Las Vegas Metropolitan Police Officer Brady Cook | October 4, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead) POTUS AND VP TODAY Today, Vice President Pence hosted the first meeting of the National Space Council at the Steven F. Udvar-Hazy Center in Chantilly, Virginia. In the afternoon, Vice President Pence will travel to Florida to receive a briefing on hurricane recovery efforts and to tour a Disaster Relief Center. This evening, President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump will meet with senior military leaders and their spouses at the White House. COMING UP Tomorrow, the President and First Lady will host an event in honor of Hispanic Heritage Month at the White House. The Vice President will depart Florida en route to Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands to meet with FEMA and survey affected sites of Hurricane Maria.

subject: Demand Republicans Stand Up to the NRA
body:
Emergency petition: Thoughts and prayers are not enough. We need gun control now. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/las-vegas_dems)

Tell Republicans in Congress:

"It is time to put your constituents before the National Rifle Association. Reject currently pending legislation that would gut regulations on gun silencers and armor piercing bullets, block the Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act, and take immediate action to ban weapons of war, including assault weapons and large capacity magazines, which have no place in the hands of private citizens.”

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/las-vegas_dems)

Dear Activist,

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/las-vegas_dems)

Sunday night in Las Vegas, a lone gunmen rained gunfire down on a country music concert, killing more than 50 people and sending more than 400 to the hospital. It was the deadliest mass shooting in U.S. history.1

Republicans in Congress – who have fought for years to block any legislation that would protect Americans from gun violence – were quick to offer their “thoughts and prayers” in response. But thoughts and prayers are not enough to keep weapons of war out of dangerous hands and begin to address our country’s horrific gun violence epidemic.

It is time for Republicans in Congress to join Democrats in standing up to the National Rifle Association and pass commonsense gun control laws that will save American lives.

Tell Republicans in Congress: Thoughts and prayers are not enough. We need gun control now. Click here to sign the petition. (http://act.credoaction.com/sign/las-vegas_dems)

Sunday night’s shooting is far from an isolated incident. According to Newsweek’s John Haltiwanger, there have now been “273 mass shootings and over 11,000 deaths from gun violence in America” in 2017 alone.2

But instead of working to protect Americans from the gun violence epidemic, Republicans in Congress are planning to vote as soon as this week on two bills that would make Americans even more vulnerable to gun violence:

The SHARE Act (H.R. 3668), which would repeal restrictions on gun silencers and make it harder to detect the source of gunfire during mass shootings.3 The Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act (H.R. 38), which would undermine state efforts to protect residents from gun violence by forcing states to recognize concealed carry permits issued by any other state, no matter how lax the state’s permitting standards are.4
We must forcefully demand that Congress reject both of these dangerous pieces of legislation and take immediate action to ban weapons of war, including assault weapons and large capacity magazines. Please add your name to our emergency petition now.

Tell Republicans in Congress: Thoughts and prayers are not enough. We need gun control now. Click here to sign the petition. (http://act.credoaction.com/sign/las-vegas_dems)

Stephen Paddock, the suspect in the Las Vegas shooting, reportedly had at least 10 rifles in the hotel room he used as a sniper perch.6 There’s simply no good reason for a private citizen to have access to that many weapons of war, but it should not come as a surprise.

Nevada has some of the weakest gun control laws in the United States. There are no limits on the number of guns an individual can own, and assault weapons are legal in the state as long as they are registered.5 The combination of weak state and federal gun laws made Sunday night’s horrific attack possible.

There’s one reason why Congress consistently fails to take real action on gun violence: the National Rifle Association. The NRA has a chokehold on Congress that keeps most bills about gun control from even coming to the floor for a vote. Politicians beholden to – or afraid of – the NRA are willing to turn their backs on their constituents when it comes time to implement reasonable limits and controls on guns. But you can count on them for a “heartbroken” tweet about their “thoughts and prayers” when a tragic shooting makes the national news.

Finally breaking the NRA’s chokehold on Congress will require massive grassroots pressure on our elected officials, demanding that they deliver more than thoughts and prayers in the face of our epidemic of gun violence.

Tell Republicans in Congress: Thoughts and prayers are not enough. We need gun control now. Click the link below to sign the petition:

http://act.credoaction.com/sign/las-vegas_dems (http://act.credoaction.com/sign/las-vegas_dems)

Thanks for everything you do.

Murshed Zaheed, Political Director
CREDO Action from Working Assets (http://www.credoaction.com)

Add your name:

Democrats love to call themselves "feminists" but the second a woman doesn't fall in line with their liberal ideology, she's attacked, silenced, and subjected to the very same sexist attacks liberals pretend to fight.

You are receiving this email at dpwillis67@gmail.com Republican National Committee (RNC), 310 1st St SE Washington, DC, 20003-1885, US We believe this is an important way to reach our grassroots supporters with the most up-to-date information regarding the efforts of the Republican Party and President Trump, and we're glad you're on our team. It's because of grassroots supporters like you that we will Make America Great Again, and we appreciate your support. Thank you for all that you do! | |

name: must-see@dscc.org
subject: Special message from Joe Biden
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
Joe Biden has an important message to share about the upcoming special election in Alabama. Doug Jones is counting on us to help him compete against Roy Moore >>
---
**[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/take-on-republicans-everywhere?refcode=DPE-20171005-MustSee_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**
Team --

Joe Biden knows the importance of this Alabama special election -- that's why he went to Birmingham this week to speak at a rally for Democratic nominee Doug Jones.

And we wanted to make sure you saw what Joe Biden had to say this week:

> _Look, I 've campaigned for a lot of candidates over the years... and I can count on two hands the people I've campaigned for who have as much integrity, courage, and sense of honor and duty that Doug Jones has.
>
> For 40 years justice had gone undone. The 16th Street Baptist Church. He helped remove 40 years of stain and pain. 40 years...Doug said no more. The Klan needed to know that justice would follow them to the gates of hell if need be.
>
> Justice was delayed but it could not be denied. And anyone who knew this man was not at all surprised...Let's get him in the Senate...I'm asking._
Let's stand with Joe and give Doug the strong start he needs in this special election by giving him the resources he needs to take on GOP nominee Roy Moore. Are you in?

[**Chip in $1 right now to answer Joe Biden 's call to action -- and help show the GOP that grassroots Democrats are ALL IN to fight for every seat in 2018.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/take-on-republicans-everywhere?refcode=DPE-20171005-MustSee_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

This will be a tough race, there's no doubt about it. But what's important at this moment is that Democrats show we are united in our support for Doug.

The Republicans just went through a bitter primary where ultra-conservative Roy Moore edged out the establishment candidate supported by Mitch McConnell and Donald Trump. Now we have the chance to deal them another blow by electing Doug to the Senate.

Let's show the GOP that Democrats will compete everywhere -- even in Alabama.

**Your contribution will allow Doug to run a competitive campaign against Roy Moore while helping to elect Democratic Senators in races across the country. Will you pitch in today?**

[**http://www.dscc.org/support-Democratic-Senate-candidates**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/take-on-republicans-everywhere?refcode=DPE-20171005-MustSee_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Thanks for showing your support for Doug and Democratic candidates everywhere.

Team DSCC

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954de33/2b07114c/e9fad919/731524fe/4216302000/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. name: Jason Yates, My Faith Votes
subject: Governor Mike Huckabee joins My Faith Votes
body:
| | My Faith Votes' New Honorary National Chairman.
---
[ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171005_gmh_s6&n=1) To stop receiving emails, [click here](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/unsubscribe?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171005_gmh_s6&n=2).
| Dear Peter, I am thrilled to reveal some fantastic news! Governor Mike Huckabee will succeed Dr. Ben Carson as the new Honorary National Chairman of My Faith Votes. We could not be more excited to welcome the former Arkansas governor to the My Faith Votes team. Gov. Huckabee is a champion of Judeo-Christian values and easily is one of the most influential Christians in politics. He brings the unique perspective not only as a former governor and presidential candidate but also the experience of a Pastor who is passionate to see the church bring faith into the public square. **_“My goal is simple: to see every single Christian in America show up during every single election to make their voices heard. I’m proud to join My Faith Votes.” - Gov. Huckabee_** Governor Huckabee said it best, “I’ve never been more convinced of this: Christians will determine every election in America if they just show up. We’ve reached a tipping point in America’s history, and we’ll either emerge from it stronger and better than ever before, or we’ll slide into an irreversible decline. It’s time for people of faith to step up to the plate and lead our nation.” | | [Show your support for Gov. Huckabee ►](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fhuckabee-learn-more&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171005_gmh_s6&n=3)
---
If you want to get to know Gov. Huckabee better, tune into his new TV show premiering on the Trinity Broadcast Network (TBN) October 7th at 8/7c, when his first guest will be President Donald Trump. His show, as Gov. Huckabee says, will be a “big old front porch where we can all meet.”
We are blessed to have [Gov. Huckabee’s bold and faithful leadership at My Faith Votes](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fhuckabee-learn-more&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171005_gmh_s6&n=4) as we enter into the fall election season with the midterm elections right around the corner.
Together, as Christians, we stand united to see America stand strong for God.
Standing together,

Jason Yates
CEO
My Faith Votes
_My Faith Votes is a non-partisan movement focused on motivating Christians in America to participate in local and national elections and be active members of society. By partnering with local churches, pastors, and national faith leaders, My Faith Votes mobilizes and equips Christians to lead the conversation on the place of faith in culture and politics._
* * *

Friend,

Yesterday, my campaign announced that in the first three weeks I was in the U.S. Senate race, I raised nearly $250,000. That’s no small feat, and I couldn't have done it without the help of people like you.

**Thank you!**

As I’ve traveled from Burnett County to Kenosha County the last few weeks, I see momentum already growing for our campaign.

People tell me they want a Senator who is a proven, conservative leader with a solid record of getting things done. We can’t let DC dysfunction get in the way of the important reforms we need right now, and we need someone we can trust to take the Wisconsin Way to Washington.

If you’re with me, and you want bold conservative grit in Washington, please join the team!

What are the [issues](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=9eb56a6655bf401083593140f9ec9076&_e=GTrpw7hbGjv7iVW8DI3Qz4rz85ZxI9YRZIStaPGQVCRuQiOWj0WB6eFF5hBvqxpmZV1YcM6l-ztLFR0dH7dZNNPixVPcN4jrYKUZtEoO1-vXijY_zEtp5ygyK5hQ4uJt5ZzXvlh8nxj-3bOeO1pdfz_QkD4GDIQGGkLEgcLdf4TuShWl_XkXDGdZ4hmMCIB0go1wFSESzSSN1bKGcdNgVsMAuV8TlQgWKru2zNN_oKQ6bFGluYWDPcbGTAm2mlWbUyQdlOMUuh4ACzbzELHjHIizFxgtEHgShabwRUCNwy-Lk46kfTO6-qKOCH5K7rbKScxIYaw3rA84XQ9LKVSdSi77LpoQi2M6J6g-AZUm-pczKsy5meeKZkaQ54Iew9N5) that you care about? name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: SCOTUS heard an important case Tuesday
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

The Republicans have successfully installed a system in states across
America that almost guarantees them a majority of legislative seats
with a minority of votes.

That system is hyperpartisan gerrymandering -- and Tuesday it went
before the Supreme Court in the case Gill v. Whitford, which may
ultimately decide if this illegitimate form of politicians selecting
voters is constitutional or not.

The deciding vote in this case may come down to Justice Gorsuch, who
is an illegitimate presence on the Court and could well solidify this
anti-democratic process for a generation or more. After all, how can
voters change the system if they can’t change their representatives?

That’s why we must enact a 21st Century Voting Rights Act to address
hyperpartisan gerrymandering and other abuses of our electoral system.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/21st_VRA_video/?t=1002&akid=315%2E356062%2Ee4_q3Y ]Add your name to our petition supporting a 21st Century Voting
Rights Act today. Help solve hyperpartisan gerrymandering and other
abuses of our electoral system.

The idea of drawing district lines to benefit one party or the other
is not new; however, a confluence of specific events over the past
decade has made gerrymandering a lethal science to our democratic
legitimacy.

1. Citizens United unleashed record amounts of dark money into our
elections -- which Republicans used in 2010 to target strategic
local races that would enable them to control redistricting before
Democrats had adapted to the new reality.
2. The Shelby County v. Holder Supreme Court decision that gutted the
1965 Voting Rights Act in 2013 has allowed states like North
Carolina, Texas, and others to implement racially motivated and
hyperpartisan gerrymandering with less federal scrutiny.
3. Between 2000 and 2010, computers became so sophisticated they
could tailor-draw maps that all but guarantee which party will win
which district.

I’ve called for provisions ending hyperpartisan gerrymandering to be
included in the 21st Century Voting Rights Act, and supplemented by
state legislation if necessary. But right now, no one is demanding
this law -- except thousands of us, already standing up and speaking
as one.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/21st_VRA_video/?t=1003&akid=315%2E356062%2Ee4_q3Y ]Join the call for reform: Add your name to the 21st Century Voting
Rights Act today.

It’s up to us to get our representatives in Congress behind this
important set of voter protections. As the Gill v. Whitford case
unfolds before the Supreme Court, remember: there is something you and
your fellow voters can do. Your voice is valuable in this fight.

Thank you for standing up for democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold

name: Danny Glover
subject: Why I support the Sanders Institute
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Friend,
The present state of our country and of our world beckons to all of us. As we confront climate change, multiple refugee crises, the threat of global conflict, and a disturbing normalization of fascism, our collective future mandates that we unite around calls for justice with a sense of urgency - justice for women, justice for LGBTQ communities, justice for immigrants, justice for racial and ethnic minorities, justice for religious minorities, justice for the economically disenfranchised, justice for our environment. We are called to defend the self-evident truths upon which democracy is built - equality, freedom, and the ability to pursue personal fulfilment - from forces rooted in falsehood, manipulation, and demagoguery. To do so, we must inform ourselves thoroughly and organize effectively. It is in this spirit that I support the Sanders Institute in actively engaging citizens and media in the pursuit of progressive solutions to economic, environmental, racial, and social justice issues.
The Sanders Institute's focus on individuals and media speaks directly to the terrain of the digital age. Its emphasis on progressive solutions speaks to our collective need to defend our highest ideals by effecting positive change. While mendacity can be a shortcut to power, that power is ultimately unsustainable. We must speak powerful truths to power; truths rooted in our diversity and interconnectedness. In recognizing the ways in which we all have something to contribute and the ways in which we all depend on one another, we harness the value of our differences to establish powerful coalitions; coalitions that can effectively counter the rigidity and isolation of illiberalism. As a Fellow of the Sanders Institute, I offer my experience in supporting social justice movements around the world on issues like environmental justice, labor, economic inequality, and racism, and I hope to inspire a new generation of socially engaged citizens in fighting for justice and equality for all.
Danny Glover, Sanders Institute Founding Fellow, 2017

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Moving past the “this isn't the time” crowd
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
I'm calling on the state legislature to ban the devices that turn semi-automatic weapons into fully automatic weapons.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTIwODM3OTgS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDQyODY3NwS2&mt=1&rt=0) Friend, There are few answers from Sunday night in Las Vegas but one thing we do know is that fewer people would have died or been injured if the gunman had been unable to buy a bump stock -- which converts a semi-automatic weapon into a fully automatic weapon. Not only do we need to ban assault weapons, we need to ban the devices that turn semi-automatic weapons into yet more deadly fully automatic weapons. I imagine you're with me here: I'm beyond sick and tired of Republicans leaders who take millions from the NRA and then turn around and say "now isn't the time to talk about gun safety solutions." It wasn't the time after Orlando. It wasn't the time after Sandy Hook. It wasn't the time after San Bernardino. And now, after Las Vegas, we're told it's still not the time. The gun lobby, their friends in Congress, and their commitment to stopping any bipartisan conversations about gun safety, are threatening our safety. Period. So I'm not waiting for them. I didn't wait in 1994 when I took a decisive vote to ban assault weapons. And I'm not waiting now as, once again, we are mourning the violent loss of innocent lives in Las Vegas. That's why I'm calling on the state legislature here in Washington to ban the devices that turn semi-automatic weapons into yet more deadly fully-automatic weapons. It's just one step -- but it's an important one. Anything we can do to stop even one act of gun violence is worth doing -- and stopping the unfettered access to military-style weaponry is darn worth doing. [**Are you with me? Add your name right now to support this legislation and show our closely divided legislature the gun lobby does not win in Washington.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIwODM3OTgS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDQyODY3NwS2&mt=1&rt=0) Gun violence is a massive problem in our country and will require many solutions. One reform won't necessarily prevent every act of violence. But not trying, not doing something, is an abdication of leadership. Banning bump stocks and other devices will put up a major barrier to turning legal semi-automatics into fully automatic weapons -- it's just common sense. There are plenty of folks telling us we shouldn't talk about gun safety after a terrible moment of gun violence -- I couldn't disagree more. I'm taking action. I'm fighting for solutions. Our country, our citizens, our future deserves nothing less. Thanks for being there with me. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTIwODM3OTgS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDQyODY3NwS2&mt=1&rt=0)

name: Shailene Woodley, Our Revolution
subject: Fwd: Join Me, October 10th, for a Livestream On College for All.
body:
Today, a college degree is the equivalent of what a high school diploma was
#
Friends,
College affordability is one of the great moral crises of our time, with millions of bright, capable people priced out of an education and millions more in debt that totals over one and a half trillion dollars for our country.
[**RSVP to join Senator Bernie Sanders for a College for All Livestream on Tuesday, October 10 at 7 p.m. ET.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af0a/bed43eb/1d16fef6/75ffee1e/1149623786/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/rsvp-for-c4a-livestream?source=em171005")
Together we can guarantee that everyone, no matter their circumstances, can receive the highest quality of education without being priced out or burdened by student loan debt.
In solidarity,
Shailene Woodley
Board Member
Our Revolution
\----------Forwarded message----------
From: Bernie Sanders
Subject: Join Me, October 10th, for a Livestream On College for All
The world is changing, sisters and brothers.
Today, a college degree is the equivalent of what a high school diploma was a generation ago, and competing effectively in the global economy requires the best-educated workforce possible.
And what is insane to me, is that in the richest country in the history of the world, we have hundreds of thousands of bright young people who are unable to go to college because they can't afford it. At the same time, we must also end the absurdity of millions of people who leave college and graduate school drowning with debt that hangs around their necks for years, or even decades.
In my opinion, at a time of such massive income and wealth inequality, we must demand that all of our people, regardless of their economic background, have the opportunity to pursue their educational dreams, which will make our economy more productive.
On October 10, 2017, I am giving a speech at Castleton University in Castleton, Vermont about my idea to make tuition free at public colleges and universities and substantially reduce student debt. The speech will be livestreamed and my hope is that students will organize meetings on college campuses across the country to watch and talk about the event.
Here is the simple truth. If students and faculty around the country stand up and demand that public colleges and universities become tuition free, there is no question that we will win that fight. Please stand with us and say that you'll join us:
[**I will join the College for All livestream on October 10 at 7 p.m. ET.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af0a/bed43eb/1d16fef6/75ffee1e/1149623786/VEsE/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/rsvp-for-c4a-livestream?source=em171005")
**[SIGN UP»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af0a/bed43eb/1d16fef6/75ffee1e/1149623786/VEsF/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/rsvp-for-c4a-livestream?source=em171005")**
During our presidential campaign, we asked people how their lives would change if we were able to make college tuition free while reducing rates on existing student loans.
Here were a few of the answers we received:
"Free tuition means that my talented, intelligent granddaughter could go to college. She was accepted at several schools, but none of them offered enough funding for us to afford it. Now she feels like her dream went up in smoke." - Carrie from Massachusetts
"Free tuition would mean I could actually attend college, something that is out of reach for me currently. My family is broke (my parents filed for bankruptcy a few years ago), and I refuse to apply for loans that will stay with me for the rest of my life." - Holly from New Hampshire
"If college tuition were free, my son would be able to go to college without having to worry about being crushed under huge student loan debt. I would also love to take classes, as my parents couldn't afford it for me either. If there was a way to refinance loans, my husband's student loan debt, which is in the tens of thousands, would become much easier for us to manage. If other countries can do it, so can we." - Bonnie from Washington
"As the parent of two kids, ages 11 and 14, it would eliminate the terror I'm feeling, wondering how we're going to pay for college for our kids." - Emily from New York
When we talk about public education in this country, it is not just 1st grade through 12th that we should be discussing. It is time for the United States to make public colleges and universities tuition free.
That is what we'll be talking about on college campuses across the country on October 10, and I hope that you're willing to join our livestream to help us make that happen:
[**Yes, I will join the College for All livestream on October 10 at 7 p.m. ET.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af0a/bed43eb/1d16fef6/75ffee1e/1149623786/VEsC/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/rsvp-for-c4a-livestream?source=em171005")
Together, we can revolutionize how higher education is funded, and lower the oppressive burden of debt that so many carry in this country.

In solidarity,

Senator Bernie Sanders

603 2ND ST, NE
WASHINGTON, DC, 20002
